# ADR-XXX: <Title>

> Format: Michael Nygard. Keep concise — one page preferred, two pages max.

## Status

Proposed | Accepted | Superseded by ADR-YYY | Deprecated

## Date

YYYY-MM-DD

## Context

What is the issue being addressed? What are the forces at play (technical, political, project, team)? State the context in 2-4 short paragraphs.

Avoid naming decisions here — that's the Decision section's job. Just describe the problem.

## Decision

One sentence describing what was decided. Imperative voice ("Use X", "Require Y", "Ban Z").

Then 1-2 paragraphs explaining what the decision commits us to concretely. Include code / config references when applicable.

## Alternatives considered

- **Option A**: why rejected.
- **Option B**: why rejected.
- **Option C** (named fallback): kept as an explicit Should-Have trim if the primary decision stalls.

## Consequences

### Positive

- ...

### Negative

- ...

### Neutral

- ...

## Amendments

Once the ADR is Accepted, further changes go here (with date + rationale) rather than rewriting the original Decision section.

### YYYY-MM-DD — <short title>

...

## References

- [External source 1](https://...)
- [[Vault note 1]] — if citing an Obsidian note
- `./adr-00X.md` — related ADR
- `.claude/rules/<rule>.md` — related project rule
